title: Chown in copier.go passes uid for both uid/gid
body:
### Issue Description

I work as a developer on z/OS, and part of my job involves upgrading the version of podman we provide on z/OS. Recently, I noticed some code in buildah that I believe to be an issue. Specifically here -https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/copier/copier.go#L2408
```
if err = chown(filepath.Join(req.Root, leaf), uid, uid); err != nil {
	return errorResponse("copier: ensure: error setting owner of %q to %d:%d: %v", leaf, uid, gid, err)
}
```
I think it should be passing in `uid, gid` instead of `uid, uid` since they could be different. Maybe this was just a typo since uid is so similar to gid? Or is there something I'm missing?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

N/A


### Describe the results you received

N/A

### Describe the results you expected

N/A

comments.body: Yup, that's definitely a typo.  Would you like to open the pull request?  Otherwise I can.

> Yup, that's definitely a typo. Would you like to open the pull request? Otherwise I can.

Sure I will open a PR - thanks for taking a look.
